name: InNat
comment:
Operations when you know the `maximum` of the `Nat (In minimum maximum maybeN)`.

For example for the argument in

    toHexChar : Nat (In min Nat15 maybeN) -> Char

you should use `InNat` operations.

If the maximum isn't known, use the operations in `MinNat`.


## add information


### clamp

@docs atMost, atLeast


### compare

@docs is, isInRange, isAtLeast, isAtMost


## modify

@docs addN, subN, add, sub

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (In ...)`.

    NNat.toIn nat3
        |> InNat.add between1And12 nat1 nat12
    --> of type Nat (In Nat4 (Nat15Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In addedMin addedMax addedMaybeMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMin (Nat.Bound.Is min Nat.Bound.To sumMin) x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMax (Nat.Bound.Is max Nat.Bound.To sumMax) y) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn sumMin sumMax)

values.name: addN
values.comment:
Add a fixed `Nat (N ...)` value.

    nat70 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.addN nat7
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat77 (Nat77Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N added (Nat.Bound.Is min Nat.Bound.To sumMin) (Nat.Bound.Is max Nat.Bound.To sumMax)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn sumMin sumMax)

values.name: atLeast
values.comment:
If the `Nat (In ...)` is less than a number, return that number instead.

    nat5 |> InNat.atLeast nat10
    --> Nat 10

    nat15 |> InNat.atLeast nat10
    --> Nat 15
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In newMin max lowerMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn newMin max)

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the `Nat (In ...)` to at most a number.

    nat5 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> InNat 5

    nat15 |> NNat.toIn |> Nat.lowerMin nat5
        |> InNat.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> Nat 10

`min` ensures that that number is at least the minimum.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minNewMax atLeastNewMax newMaxMaybeN) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToMinNewMax Nat.Bound.To minNewMax) x) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min atLeastNewMax)

values.name: is
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried (Nat.Bound.Is triedToMax Nat.Bound.To max) (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To (N.Nat1Plus atLeastTriedMinus1))) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToTried Nat.Bound.To tried) x) } -> { equal : () -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min atLeastTriedMinus1 maybeN) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat2Plus triedMinus1) max maybeN) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `Nat` or

  - `less`?

```
vote : { age : Nat (In (Nat18Plus orOlder) max) } -> Vote

tryToVote =
    Nat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isAtLeast nat18
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = Nothing --😓
            , equalOrGreater = \age -> Just (vote { age = age })
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To (N.Nat1Plus atLeastTriedMinus1)) (Nat.Bound.Is atLeastRange Nat.Bound.To max)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is (N.Nat1Plus lessRange) Nat.Bound.To tried) x) } -> { less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min atLeastTriedMinus1 maybeN) -> result, equalOrGreater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In tried max maybeN) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `equalOrLess` than a `Nat` or

  - `greater`?

```
goToU18Party : { age : Nat (In min Nat17) } -> List Snack

tryToGoToU18Party =
    Nat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isAtMost nat17
            { min = nat0 }
            { equalOrLess = \age -> Just (goToU18Party { age = age })
            , greater = Nothing
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To atLeastTried) (Nat.Bound.Is (N.Nat1Plus greaterRange) Nat.Bound.To max)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToTried Nat.Bound.To tried) x) } -> { equalOrLess : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min atLeastTried maybeN) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat1Plus tried) max maybeN) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: isInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range `first` to `last`, is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `inRange`

  - `greater` than the `last` or

  - `less` than the `first`?

```
justIfBetween3And10 =
    Nat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isInRange { first = nat3, last = nat10 }
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> Nothing
            , greater = \_ -> Nothing
            , inRange = Just
            }

justIfBetween3And10 nat9
--> Just (Nat 9)

justIfBetween3And10 nat123
--> Nothing
```
values.type: { first : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N first (Nat.Bound.Is firstToLast Nat.Bound.To last) (Nat.Bound.Is firstA Nat.Bound.To (N.Nat1Plus atLeastFirstMinus1))), last : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N last (Nat.Bound.Is lastToMax Nat.Bound.To max) (Nat.Bound.Is lastA Nat.Bound.To atLeastLast)) } -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToFirst Nat.Bound.To first) x) } -> { inRange : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In first atLeastLast maybeN) -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min atLeastFirstMinus1 maybeN) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat1Plus last) max maybeN) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: sub
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ...)`.

The 2 following arguments are

  - the minimum subtracted value

  - the maximum subtracted value

    in6To12
    |> InNat.sub in1And5 nat1 nat5
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat1 (Nat5Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minSubbed maxSubbed subbedMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N minSubbed (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMax Nat.Bound.To max) x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N maxSubbed (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMin Nat.Bound.To min) y) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn differenceMin differenceMax)

values.name: subN
values.comment:
Subtract a fixed `Nat` value.

    nat7 |> NNat.toIn
        |> InNat.subN nat7
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat0 a)

**Use [`MinNat.subN`](MinNat#subN) if the maximum value is `Infinity`**.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N sub (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMin Nat.Bound.To min) (Nat.Bound.Is differenceMax Nat.Bound.To max)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn differenceMin differenceMax)

name: MinNat
comment:
2 situations where you use these operations instead of the ones in [`Nat`](Nat) or [`InNat`](InNat):

1.  Your value is of type `Nat (ValueMin ...)`

        nat3 |> Nat.mul nat3
        --> Nat (ValueMin Nat3)

2.  The maximum is a type variable

        divideBy : Nat (In (Nat1Plus minMinus1) max maybeN) -> --...
        divideBy atLeast1 =
            --...


### compare

@docs is, isAtLeast, isAtMost


### modify

@docs subN, add, sub, addN

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (ValueMin ...)`. The second argument is the minimum if the added `Nat (ValueMin ...)`.

    atLeast5 |> MinNat.add atLeast2 nat2
    --> is of type Nat (ValueMin Nat7)

If you have don't the minimum added value at hand, use [`InNat.addLossy`](InNat#addLossy).

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (ValueMin ...)`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `Nat` or

  - `less`?

```
factorial : MinNat min -> MinNat Nat1
factorial =
    MNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> MinNat.isAtLeast nat1
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> nat1 |> Nat.toMin
            , equalOrGreater =
                \atLeast1 ->
                    atLeast1
                        |> Nat.mul
                            (factorial
                                (atLeast1 |> MinNat.subN nat1)
                            )
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In triedMin (N.Nat1Plus triedMinMinus1PlusA) triedMaybeN) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min (Nat.Bound.Is minToTriedMin Nat.Bound.To triedMin) x) } -> { less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min triedMinMinus1PlusA) -> result, equalOrGreater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin triedMin) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> result

values.name: sub
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ...)`. The second argument is the maximum of the subtracted `Nat (In ...)`.

    atLeast6 |> MinNat.sub in0To5 nat5
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat1 (Nat6Plus a))

If you have don't the maximum subtracted value at hand, use [`subLossy`](InNat#subLossy).

name: NNat

values.name: add
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus another `Nat (N ...)`. Give the added value twice as a tuple.

    nat6 |> NNat.add ( nat5, nat5 )
    --> Nat 11 of type
    --> Nat
    -->     (ValueN Nat11
    -->         (Nat11Plus a)
    -->         (Is a To Nat11Plus a)
    -->         (Is b To Nat11Plus b)
    -->     )

This is only rarely useful, as you shouldn't

    inRangeXAndXPlus10 x =
        -- won't work
        isInRange x (x |> NNat.add ( nat10, nat10 ))

    isAtLeast10GreaterThan x =
        -- the only use case
        isAtLeast (x |> NNat.add ( nat10, nat10 ))

(examples don't compile, just for demonstration)
values.type: ( T.Nat (Nat.Bound.N added (Nat.Bound.Is n Nat.Bound.To sum) (Nat.Bound.Is atLeastN Nat.Bound.To atLeastSum)), T.Nat (Nat.Bound.N added (Nat.Bound.Is aPlusN Nat.Bound.To aPlusSum) (Nat.Bound.Is bPlusN Nat.Bound.To bPlusSum)) ) -> T.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueN n atLeastN (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To aPlusN) (Nat.Bound.Is b Nat.Bound.To bPlusN)) -> T.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueN sum atLeastSum (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To aPlusSum) (Nat.Bound.Is b Nat.Bound.To bPlusSum))

values.name: sub
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus another `Nat (N ...)`. Give the added value twice as a tuple.

    nat6 |> NNat.sub ( nat5, nat5 )
    --> Nat 1 of type
    --> Nat
    -->     (N Nat1
    -->         (Is a To Nat1Plus a)
    -->         (Is b To Nat1Plus b)
    -->     )

This is only rarely useful, as you shouldn't

    inRangeXMinus10ToX x =
        -- won't work
        isInRange (x |> NNat.sub ( nat10, nat10 )) x

    isAtLeast10LessThan x =
        -- the only use case
        isAtLeast (x |> NNat.sub ( nat10, nat10 ))

(examples don't compile, just for demonstration)
values.type: ( T.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subbed (Nat.Bound.Is difference Nat.Bound.To n) (Nat.Bound.Is atLeastDifference Nat.Bound.To atLeastN)), T.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subbed (Nat.Bound.Is aPlusDifference Nat.Bound.To aPlusN) (Nat.Bound.Is bPlusDifference Nat.Bound.To bPlusN)) ) -> T.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueN n atLeastN (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To aPlusN) (Nat.Bound.Is b Nat.Bound.To bPlusN)) -> T.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueN difference atLeastDifference (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To aPlusDifference) (Nat.Bound.Is b Nat.Bound.To bPlusDifference))

values.name: toIn
values.comment:
Convert it to a `Nat (ValueIn min max)`.

    nat4 |> NNat.toIn
    --> is of type Nat (ValueIn Nat4 (Nat4Plus a))

There is **only 1 situation you should use this.**

To make these the same type.

    [ in3To10, nat3 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (ValueIn Nat3)`

    [ in3To10
    , nat3 |> NNat.toIn
    ]
values.type: T.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> T.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min max)

name: Nat

aliases.name: Nat
aliases.comment:
A **bounded** natural number (`>= 0`).


## value / return types

    -- >= 4
    Nat (ValueMin Nat4)

    -- 2 <= nat <= 12
    Nat (ValueIn Nat2 Nat12)

    -- = 3, & 3, described as a difference
    Nat
        (ValueN Nat3
            (Nat3Plus more)
            (Is a To (Nat3Plus a))
            (Is b To (Nat3Plus b))
        )


## function argument types

    -- >= 4
    Nat (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4) max maybeN)

    -- 4 <= nat <= 15
    Nat (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4) Nat15 maybeN)

    -- An exact number nTo15 away from 15
    Nat (N n (Is nTo15 To Nat15) x)

    -- any, just >= 0
    Nat range

    -- Not constuctable
    Nat Banana

values.name: abs
values.comment:
The absolute value of an `Int`, which is at least `Nat0`.

    Nat.abs 16 --> Nat 16

    Nat.abs -4 --> Nat 4

Really only use this if you want the absolute value.

    badLength list =
        List.length >> Nat.abs

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                MinNat.addN nat1
                    >> Nat.lowerMin nat0
            )
            (nat0 |> Nat.toMin)

If something like this isn't possible, use [`MinNat.intAtLeast`](MinNat#intAtLeast)!
values.type: Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin N.Nat0)

values.name: bi
values.comment:
Use the `Int` values of two `Nat`s to return a result.

    Nat.bi (>=) nat5 nat4 --> True

    Nat.bi (>=) nat5 nat40 --> False

Note, that you must give the `Nat`s in the same order you would give `Int`s.

Don't overuse this.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> result) -> Nat.Nat aRange -> Nat.Nat bRange -> result

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `Nat (In ...)`.

  - `/ 0` is impossible

  - `x / d` is at most x

```
atMost7
    |> Nat.div nat3
--> Nat 2 of type Nat (ValueIn Nat0 (Nat7Plus a))
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat1Plus divMinMinus1) divMax divMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn N.Nat0 max)

values.name: intAtLeast
values.comment:
A `Nat (Min ...)` from an `Int`; if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned.

    9 |> Nat.intAtLeast nat3
    --> Nat 9

    0 |> Nat.intAtLeast nat3
    --> Nat 3

You can also use this if you know an `Int` must be at least `minimum`.

But avoid it if you can do better, like

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                MinNat.addN nat1
                    >> Nat.lowerMin nat0
            )
            (nat0 |> Nat.toMin)

If you want to handle the case `< minimum` yourself, use [`Nat.isIntAtLeast`](Nat#isIntAtLeast).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

values.name: intInRange
values.comment:
A `Nat (In ...)` from an `Int`, **clamped** between a minimum & maximum.

  - if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned
  - if the `Int > maximum`, `maximum` is returned

```
clampBetween3And12 =
    Nat.intInRange nat3 nat12

9 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 9

0 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 3

99 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 12
```

If you want to handle the cases `< minimum` & `> maximum` explicitly, use [`isIntInRange`](InNat#isIntInRange).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min firstMax maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMax max upperMaybeN) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn min max)

values.name: isIntAtLeast
values.comment:
If the `Int >= a minimum`, `Just` the `Nat (Min minimum)`, else `Nothing`.

    4 |> Nat.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Nothing

    1234 |> Nat.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Just (Nat 1234)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Basics.Int -> Maybe.Maybe (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min))

values.name: isIntInRange
values.comment:
An `Int` compared to a range from `first` to `last`.

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt =
        Nat.isIntInRange nat1 nat100
            { less = Err "must be >= 1"
            , greater = \_-> Err "must be <= 100"
            , inRange = Ok
            }

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt 0
    --> Err "must be >= 1"
values.type: { first : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minFirst last firstMaybeN), last : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In last lastPlusA lastMaybeN) } -> { less : () -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin (N.Nat1Plus last)) -> result, inRange : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn minFirst lastPlusA) -> result } -> Basics.Int -> result

values.name: lowerMin
values.comment:
Set the minimum lower.

    [ atLeast3, atLeast4 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (ValueMin Nat3)`

    [ atLeast3
    , atLeast4 |> MNat.lowerMin nat3
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lowerMin min lowerMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn lowerMin max)

values.name: maxIs
values.comment:
Make it fit into functions with require a higher maximum.

You should design type annotations as general as possible.

    onlyAtMost18 : Nat (In min Nat18 maybeN)

    onlyAtMost18 between3And8 --fine

But once you implement `onlyAtMost18`, you might use the value in `onlyAtMost19`.

    onlyAtMost18 value =
        -- onlyAtMost19 value --error :(
        onlyAtMost19
            (value |> Nat.maxIs nat18 {- works :) -})

[`lowerMin`](Nat#lowerMin) is also handy in those situations.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N max (Nat.Bound.Is a Nat.Bound.To atLeastMax) x) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min atLeastMax maybeN)

values.name: mul
values.comment:
Multiply by a `Nat (In ...)` >= 1.
we know that if `a >= 1`, `x * a >= x`.

    atLeast5  |> Nat.mul nat2
    --> Nat 10 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> Nat.mul nat5
    --> Nat 10 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat2)

The maximum value of both factors can be `Infinity`.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat1Plus minMultipliedMinus1) maxMultiplied multipliedMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

values.name: random
values.comment: Generate a random `Nat (ValueIn ...)` in a range.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin firstMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lastMin lastMax lastMaybeN) -> Random.Generator (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn firstMin lastMax))

values.name: range
values.comment:
`Nat (In ...)`s from a first to a last value.

    from3To10 : List (Nat (ValueIn Nat3 (Nat10Plus a)))
    from3To10 =
        Nat.range nat3 nat10

    from3ToAtLeast10 : List (Nat (ValueMin Nat3))
    from3ToAtLeast10 =
        Nat.range nat3 atLeast10

The resulting `List` always has at least 1 element.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin firstMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lastMin lastMax lastMaybeN) -> List.List (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn firstMin lastMax))

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after division.

  - `% 0` is impossible
  - `x % d` is at most `x`

```
atMost7 |> Nat.remainderBy nat3
--> Nat 1 of type Nat (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a))
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat1Plus divMinMinus1) divMax divMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueIn N.Nat0 max)

values.name: theGreater
values.comment:
The greater of 2 `Nat`s.

    Nat.theGreater between1And3 (nat4 |> Nat.lowerMin nat1)
    --> Nat 4
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: theSmaller
values.comment:
The smaller of 2 `Nat`s.

    Nat.theSmaller nat3 (nat4 |> Nat.lowerMin nat3)
    --> Nat 3
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: toInt
values.comment:
Convert a `Nat` to an `Int`.

    nat4 |> Nat.toInt --> 4
    nat4 |> NNat.toIn |> Nat.toInt --> 4
    nat4 |> Nat.toMin |> Nat.toInt --> 4

    compare : Nat range -> Nat range -> Order
    compare a b =
        compare (Nat.toInt a) (Nat.toInt b)
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Basics.Int

values.name: toMin
values.comment:
Convert an exact `Nat (In min ...)` to a `Nat (ValueMin min)`.

    in4To10 |> Nat.toMin
    --> is of type Nat (ValueMin Nat4)

There is **only 1 situation you should use this.**

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1, in1To10 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (ValueMin Nat1)`

    [ atLeast1
    , in1To10 |> Nat.toMin
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

values.name: toPower
values.comment:
The `Nat (ValueMin ...) ^ a Nat (ValueMin ...)`.
We know that if `a >= 1  →  x ^ a >= x`

    atLeast5 |> Nat.toPower nat2
    --> Nat 25 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> Nat.toPower nat5
    --> Nat 25 of type Nat (ValueMin Nat2)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (N.Nat1Plus powMinMinus1) powMax powMaybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max maybeN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.ValueMin min)

name: Nat.Bound

aliases.name: In
aliases.comment:
`In minimum maximum maybeN`: Somewhere within a minimum & maximum.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

Note: maximum >= minimum for every existing `Nat (In min max ...)`:

    percent : Nat (In min Nat100 maybeN) -> Percent

→ `min <= Nat100`

If you want a number where you just care about the minimum, leave the `max` as a type _variable_.

       ↓ minimum    ↓ maximum or  →
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓...

Any natural number:

    Nat (In min max maybeN)

A number, at least 5:

    Nat (In (Nat5Plus minMinus5) max maybeN)

  - `max` could be a maximum value if there is one

  - `maybeN` could contain extra information for `N ...` if the number was exact

aliases.name: Is
aliases.comment:
`Is a To b`: an exact value as the diffference `b - a`.

    N Nat5
        (Nat5Plus more)
        (Is myAge To sistersAge)
        (Is mothersAge To fathersAge)

  - `myAge + 5 = sistersAge`
  - `mothersAge + 5 = fathersAge`

aliases.name: N
aliases.comment:
Expect an exact value.
[`InNat.addN`](InNat#addN) for example also uses the knowledge that the number is exact to describe the number as differences between other type variables.

    addN :
        Nat
            (N
                added
                (Is min To sumMin)
                (Is max To sumMax)
            )
        -> Nat (In min max)
        -> Nat (In sumMin sumMax)

You can just ignore the second difference if you don't need it ([`MinNat.addN`](MinNat#addN)).

    addN :
        Nat (N added (Is min To sumMin) x)
        -> Nat (In min max maybeN)
        -> Nat (ValueMin sumMin)

aliases.name: Only
aliases.comment:
Just the exact number.

Only useful as a function **argument** type.

Every `In NatXYZ (NatXYZPlus a) maybeN` is a `Only NatXYZ maybeN`.

    byte : Arr (Only maybeN Nat8) Bit -> Byte

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/) must have _exact 8_ `Bit`s.

`Only` is useful for [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/)s,
but you will never need it in combination with `Nat`s.

aliases.name: To
aliases.comment:
No special meaning.

    Is a To b

→ distance `b - a`.

aliases.name: ValueIn
aliases.comment:
`ValueIn minimum maximum`: A value somewhere within a minimum & maximum. We don't know the exact value, though.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

To be used as a return type, **not** as a function argument.

A number between 3 and 5

    Nat (ValueIn Nat3 (Nat5Plus a))

A number, at least 5:

    Nat (ValueIn Nat5 (Nat5Plus a))

aliases.name: ValueMin
aliases.comment:
Only **value / return types should be `Min`**.

Sometimes, you simply cannot compute a maximum.

    abs : Int -> Nat (ValueIn Nat0 ??)

This is where to use `Min`.

    abs : Int -> Nat (Min Nat0)

Every `Min min` is of type `In min ...`.

aliases.name: ValueN
aliases.comment:
The most detailed description of an exact value.

Don't use this as a function argument.

aliases.name: ValueOnly
aliases.comment:
Just the exact number.

Only useful as a **value & return** type.

    repeatOnly :
        Nat (Only n maybeN)
        -> element
        -> Arr (ValueOnly n) element

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/) must has _exactly `n`_ `element`s.

`ValueOnly` is useful for [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/)s,
but you will never need it in combination with `Nat`s.

name: TypeNats
